scenario: Zephyr Fintech upgrades a Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot service from a legacy 8.5 configuration with 16 workers processing 12000 OAuth-authenticated jobs per hour. The team still sets camunda.client.identity.scope in its YAML and must migrate to the documented 8.8 replacement listed in the deprecated camunda.client.identity table.
question: Which property does the 8.8 reference list as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.identity.scope key?

options.text: The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.identity.scope with camunda.client.auth.scope under the Spring Boot starter properties table for 8.8 services.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.identity.scope with camunda.client.identity.audience under the Spring Boot starter properties table for 8.8 services.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.identity.scope with camunda.client.auth.audience under the Spring Boot starter properties table for 8.8 services.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.identity.scope with camunda.client.cloud.scope under the Spring Boot starter properties table for 8.8 services.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.identity table points the legacy scope key to camunda.client.auth.scope with the hint that identity is now part of Camunda.

b.text: Incorrect. Audience and scope are different OAuth concerns; the documented replacement for identity.scope is camunda.client.auth.scope.

c.text: Incorrect. camunda.client.auth.audience is the replacement for identity.audience, not for identity.scope, in the 8.8 reference.

d.text: Incorrect. camunda.client.cloud.scope is not listed in the camunda.client.cloud section; scope migrates to camunda.client.auth.scope.

explanation: The deprecated camunda.client.identity table maps identity.scope to camunda.client.auth.scope. The hint column notes that identity is now part of Camunda, so OAuth properties consolidate under camunda.client.auth.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
